Daily Fantasy Value —

Hitters and probable starters ranked by projected daily value. Driven by quality-of-contact + predictive metrics, adjusted for park and regression. POC v1.

What's live (v1): xwOBA, est. wRC+, Hard-Hit%, Barrel% & Barrel/PA, Exit Velo (avg/max), Bat Speed, wOBA/SLG/OBP, BABIP and the predictive deltas (ΔwOBA, ΔBA, ΔSLG / for SP: xERA & ERA−xERA) — all from Statcast + MLB API. Park factor applied per venue. Regression nudge boosts unlucky/underperformers and dings overperformers.  Splits signal (new): the Splits column flags how today's context (vs LHP/RHP, home/away, day/night, last-7 form, and career vs the starter) tilts for or against each player — shown separately, not folded into Value. Relievers tab: bullpen arms ranked by stuff (xwOBA-against) + save/hold opportunity; Role from ESPN's depth chart; green rows = available in your league. All Starters tab = full SP pool with advanced stats + buy-low Reg, for trade targeting. Coming next: per-ballpark history. Note: wRC+ is computed from wOBA + park (FanGraphs is blocked from the server), so it tracks wOBA closely until a true source is wired.

Window:

How the ranking works

Every metric is turned into a percentile across that day's player pool. For hitters those percentiles are blended, in your priority order, into a Quality score (0–100) — move the sliders above and the list re-ranks live. Quality is multiplied by the ballpark's park factor and a regression nudge is added to give Value, the number used to rank.

For pitchers, Value starts from Pitcher Quality, then subtracts how tough the opposing lineup is and a small park penalty. Cell shading is per-column percentile: greener = better, redder = worse.

Splits are a separate signal (the columns right of Reg), z-scored so each split counts equally — deliberately not folded into Value. Not yet included: per-ballpark history.

Hitter columns

FA / green row — free agent in your ESPN league (add/drop target). Sort the FA column to group them.
Value — daily rank score = (Quality + Reg) × park factor.
Qual — quality index 0–100; weighted blend of the contact metrics below.
Reg — regression nudge (~±6). + = unlucky/underperforming his contact (buy-low); − = overperforming (regression risk).
Splits — today's matchup tilt: blends vs-hand, home/away, day/night, last-7 form and career vs today's starter (BvP), each standardized so none dominates. + = favorable day, − = unfavorable. Separate from Value.
vsHand / H/A / D/N / L7 / BvP — the pieces, as OPS vs his season norm (e.g. +.050 = 50 pts better). BvP = career vs today's starter (min 10 PA; small-sample, so read it lightly). Blank = too few PA.
xwOBA — expected wOBA from contact quality; most predictive bat metric (top weight).
wRC+* — offense vs league avg (100 = avg). *Estimated from wOBA + park.
HH% hard-hit rate (≥95 mph) · Brl% barrel rate · Brl/PA barrels per PA.
aEV / mEV — avg / max exit velocity (mph) · BatSpd avg bat speed.
wOBA / SLG / OBP — actual results so far · BABIP luck gauge on balls in play.
dwOBA / dBA / dSLG — expected minus actual; + = room to improve.
Matchup / Opp SP / Park — opponent (vs = home, @ = away), opposing starter + throwing hand, venue + park factor (100 = neutral).

Pitcher columns

Value — SP streaming score = Pitcher Quality + Reg − opponent-offense difficulty − park.
PQ — pitcher quality 0–100; blend of xwOBA-against, xERA, K%, BB%, WHIP, hard-hit/barrel allowed.
Reg — nudge from xwOBA-against and ERA vs xERA (+ = unlucky, due to improve).
Splits — today's matchup tilt for the pitcher: home/away (weight 2), vs the opposing lineup (weight 2), last-7 form (1), day/night (0.5), each standardized then weighted. + = favorable.
vsLineup / H/A / D/N / Form — the pieces; for these lower opponent OPS shows as a positive (good for the pitcher).
OppOff% — opposing lineup strength percentile; low = weak lineup = good to stream.
xwOBA-a expected wOBA allowed · xERA expected ERA from contact allowed.
K% / BB% strikeout / walk rate · ERA / WHIP actual results.
HH%a / Brl%a — hard-hit / barrel rate allowed · IP innings (sample size).

Reliever columns

For holds/saves adds — ranks bullpen arms by stuff + opportunity; sort any column.
Value = 50% Stuff + 50% Opp.
Stuff — quality index 0–100, led by xwOBA-against, plus xERA, K%, hard-hit/barrel allowed, BB%.
Opp — opportunity index 0–100 from save/hold usage (SV, SVO, GF, HLD − blown saves).
Role — CL closer · SU setup · MR+ some leverage · MR middle.
xwOBA-a / xERA stuff quality · K% / BB% · HH%a / Brl%a contact allowed.
SV / SVO / HLD / GF / BS saves, save chances, holds, games finished, blown saves · Today = today's opponent if playing.
Role reflects ESPN's reliever depth chart (auto-updated). Avail / green row / FA = free agent in your ESPN league.

All Starters (trade reference)

Every starter (GS≥4, IP≥20) with season advanced stats — not tied to today's slate. Sort any column.
PQ — pitcher quality 0–100 (xwOBA-against, xERA, K%, BB%, WHIP, hard-hit/barrel allowed). Default sort.
Reg — buy-low signal: + = unlucky / underperforming his stuff (results worse than xERA & xwOBA-against → target). − = overperforming (sell-high). Best targets = high PQ + high Reg.
ERA-xERA — raw ERA luck; + = ERA worse than deserved (buy-low).
xwOBA-a / xERA / ERA stuff & results · K% / BB% / WHIP · HH%a / Brl%a contact allowed · GS games started.

Trends tabs (model-stat deltas)

What they track — the same advanced stats the model runs on, snapshotted daily since 5/22. Each Δ column = current season-to-date value minus the value stored N days ago: + = that stat improved over the window.
Windows — toggle Δ7d / Δ15d / ΔMax in the toolbar. Max = vs the oldest stored snapshot; 15/30-day windows fill in automatically as daily history accumulates. Blank Δ = player wasn't in that day's snapshot.
Hitters: xwOBA, wRC+*, HH%, Brl%, Brl/PA, BatSpd, wOBA, SLG, OBP, BABIP (slate hitters for the viewed date). Pitchers tab covers SPs and RPs (Role column): Stuff & Opp (relievers), then xwOBA-a, xERA, K%, BB%, HH%a, Brl%a. For the against-stats a negative Δ is good (shading inverted accordingly). Default sort: hitters = biggest xwOBA gain; pitchers = biggest xwOBA-against improvement.
Cell shading: better → worse (per column). Reg / Δ columns: buy-low signal, regression risk.
